content.problemStatement:
Given an array of integers AAA, reverse the array in-place. This means you should modify the original array directly without using any extra space for another array.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer NNN, the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers representing the elements of the array AAA.


Output Format
Print the reversed array as a single line of space-separated integers.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers representing the array A.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the elements of the reversed array, space‑separated, in the same order as the test cases appear in the input.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Each test case is processed independently. The first array [1,2,3,4,5] becomes [5,4,3,2,1]; the second [10,20,30] becomes [30,20,10]; the single element array stays unchanged.
Reversing a two‑element identical array leaves it unchanged. Reversing [4,3,2,1] yields [1,2,3,4].
Maximum allowed size (15) is reversed correctly.

solution.approach: For each test case we read the array into memory. Using two indices, one starting at the beginning (l) and one at the end (r), we swap A[l] and A[r] and move l forward and r backward until they meet. This reverses the array in‑place using O(1) extra space. After reversal we print the array in order.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the size of the current array. Overall O(T·N).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space (the array itself is required for input).

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; while(T--){int n; scanf("%d",&n); int a[15]; for(int i=0;i<n;i++) scanf("%d",&a[i]); int l=0,r=n-1; while(l<r){int tmp=a[l]; a[l]=a[r]; a[r]=tmp; l++; r--;} for(int i=0;i<n;i++){printf("%d",a[i]); if(i<n-1) printf(" ");} printf("\n");} return 0; }
code.Python3:
import sys
data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
idx=0
T=int(data[idx]);idx+=1
out=[]
for _ in range(T):
    n=int(data[idx]);idx+=1
    arr=list(map(int,data[idx:idx+n]));idx+=n
    l, r = 0, n-1
    while l<r:
        arr[l],arr[r]=arr[r],arr[l]
        l+=1; r-=1
    out.append(' '.join(map(str,arr)))
print('\n'.join(out))
